title: seriphidium arbuscula subsp. longilobum
intro: Seriphidium arbuscula subsp. longilobum, also known as Artemisia longiloba, is a subspecies of the artemisia plant, a type of flowering plant in the sunflower family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations, and is also believed to have antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, Seriphidium arbuscula subsp. longilobum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional medicines and aromatherapy products.
